payload.query: Historically, the split in comparative politics has been between qualitative and quantitative methods. Emerging trends related to causal inference have encouraged scholars to focus on natural experiments, field experiments, and subnational quantitative study. What traditions does this work draw from most heavily? Should we think of causal inference as extensions of qualitative or quantitative traditions? Give examples of recent work.

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court Decision
articles.abstract: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.
